kill_fact: Could not kill it: every load-bearing fact independently confirms against wire/primary sources, and the floor is structurally sound (net-cash, liquid-Tencent NAV holdco, no going-concern/dilution/CVR). The catalyst is genuinely SOFT/DIFFUSE as the card itself discloses (no single re-rate day; the only hard date, EU Oct 11 2026, is being deliberately defanged by Prosus asking the EU to END the forced sale) - but soft is not dead: it is live, dated, and clearing favorably, so it is not REFUTED. Two corrections required.
corrections: (1) LIVE PRICE STALE: card says EUR 39.30; verified live EUR 38.22 (Jun 20 stockanalysis) / EUR 38.35 (Jun 22 intraday). Lower entry COMPRESSES the asymmetry vs the card: upside-to-target ~+20% (38.35->46.21) but downside-to-floor only ~-2.6% (38.35->37.37) - R:R is actually BETTER (~7:1 to floor) but the floor cushion is thinner than the carded -4.9%. (2) REMEDY NOT WAIVED: the EU GRANTED a deadline EXTENSION to Oct 11 2026 (Bloomberg/Reuters Jun 2), it did NOT drop the divestiture requirement. Prosus only ASKED the EU to end the forced sale (Bloomberg May 25) - request, not grant. The card's 'Prosus asking to drop the remedy' is correct; any reading that the remedy is gone is wrong - the must-sell obligation still stands, merely relaxed to Oct 11. (3) UBER EUR 33 was REJECTED by Delivery Hero and Uber is weighing a raise (~$13bn) - this STRENGTHENS the residual-stake mark vs the card's downside framing, not weakens it.
evidence: Bloomberg, 'Prosus Gets Until Oct. 11 for Delivery Hero Sale After Uber Bid' (Jun 2, 2026) + Reuters/TradingView 'Deadline for Prosus to comply with Delivery Hero stake sale extended, EU regulators say' (Jun 2, 2026) - confirms the ONLY hard date is an EXTENSION to Oct 11 2026, justified by Uber's accumulation; divestiture obligation remains in force.

CNBC 'Delivery Hero confirms takeover offer from Uber' + Reuters (May 23, 2026) - confirms Uber's EUR 33/share INDICATIVE bid (~$11bn), non-binding, BaFin/antitrust pending; subsequently rejected by DHER (Jefferies note, DHER ~EUR 37.62) and Uber considering a raise.

Prosus IR primary filings: 'Prosus sells 4.5% stake in Delivery Hero to Uber' (EUR 20.00, ~EUR 270m) and 'Prosus sells 5% interest in Delivery Hero to Aspex Management' (EUR 22.00, 15,188,284 shrs, ~EUR 335m) - confirms premium tranche sales, not a fire sale; Bloomberg (May 27) confirms Uber later bought Aspex's block (stake to 36.83%).

Bloomberg 'Prosus Asks EU to End Forced Sale of Delivery Hero Stake' (May 25, 2026) confirms the hold-optionality REQUEST; Prosus IR NAV/buyback pages confirm the floor: ~76-85% Tencent (liquid), open-ended buyback ~$42bn returned through 9/2025 (+18% NAV/shr accretion), net-cash holdco at ~30-35% NAV discount. Verified live px EUR 38.22 (Jun 20) / EUR 38.35 (Jun 22).
